mnemonics: Get the LAY of the Land: Look, Analyze, Your intuition. EDA is about exploring the landscape of your data before building anything on it.
keyPoints: The primary goal of EDA is to understand the properties of the data, detect patterns, spot anomalies, and test underlying assumptions.
It relies heavily on visual methods (plots, charts) and summary statistics (mean, median, variance).
It is an iterative process of generating questions about the data, answering them, and then refining those questions based on what you find.
commonMistakes: Skipping EDA and jumping straight to complex models, which can lead to poor performance and misinterpretation of results.
Not looking for missing values, outliers, or errors in data entry, which can severely skew analysis.
Only using summary statistics without visualizations, or vice versa; both are necessary for a complete picture.
